data.title: Gilead wins EU backing for twice-yearly HIV PrEP therapy
data.description:
Gilead (NASDAQ:GILD) announced Friday that an expert panel of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency (EMA) as part of an accelerated review, endorsed its antiviral therapy lenacapavir as a twice-yearly treatment for prevention of HIV in pre-exposure prophylaxis (PrEP).

Issuing a positive opinion, EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has recommended the injectable for use in PrEP to cut the risk of HIV-1 infection in adults and adolescents.

The recommendation for the lenacapavir marketing authorization application will next be sent to the European Commission, which is expected to make a final decision on approval before the year-end.

Gilead (NASDAQ:GILD) expects to market lenacapavir as Yeytuo across all 27 EU member states, as well as Norway, Iceland, and Liechtenstein, subject to a favorable decision from the EC.

Last month, the company received FDA approval to market the product as Yeztugo in the U.S. as part of HIV PrEP therapy for adults and adolescents.

data.title: ChoiceOne Financial Services Non-GAAP EPS of $0.91 beats by $0.13, revenue of $42.82M beats by $1.57M
data.description:
• ChoiceOne Financial Services press release (NASDAQ:COFS): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.91 beats by $0.13.
  • Revenue of $42.82M (+90.7% Y/Y) beats by $1.57M.
  • Deposits, excluding brokered deposits, declined by $98.0 million as of June 30, 2025, compared to March 31, 2025, primarily due to seasonal municipal fluctuations and some reduction of higher cost deposits acquired from the Merger.
  • Core loans, which exclude held for sale loans and loans to other financial institutions, declined by $4.8 million or less than 1% on an annualized basis during the second quarter of 2025 and grew organically by $140.1 million or 10.0% during the twelve months ended June 30, 2025.  Core loans grew by $1.4 billion due to the Merger on March 1, 2025.  
  • The provision for credit losses on loans was $650,000 in the second quarter of 2025.

data.title: Moog declares $0.29 dividend
data.description:
• Moog (NYSE:MOG.A) (NYSE:MOG.B) declared $0.29/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Payable Aug. 26; for shareholders of record Aug. 8; ex-div Aug. 8.
  • See MOG.A Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: EMA issues negative opinion on Sarepta-Roche’s DMD gene therapy
data.description:
Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) (OTCQX:RHHBF) Friday said that the European Medicines Agency has issued a negative opinion on the conditional marketing authorization for Elevidys, intended for ambulatory patients aged three to seven with Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

Roche said it will continue its dialogue with the EMA to explore a potential path forward to make Elevidys available to individuals living with DMD in the EU.

In related development, Roche is pausing shipments of Elevidys in some countries, following partner Sarepta Therapeutics’ (NASDAQ:SRPT) move in the U.S., as safety concerns mount over the Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) gene therapy.

Following three patient deaths from liver failure linked to Elevidys and another gene therapy developed by the company, Sarepta (NASDAQ:SRPT) on Monday paused U.S. shipments of the drug, approved by the FDA for the neuromuscular disorder Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

Sarepta Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SRPT) meanwhile resumed its recent sell-off on Friday after Endpoints News reported that the FDA would require the company to conduct new studies to address safety concerns and reintroduce its gene therapy, Elevidys, developed in partnership with Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY).

data.title: Moog Non-GAAP EPS of $2.37 beats by $0.22, revenue of $971M beats by $49.25M
data.description:

data.title: StellarOne GAAP EPS of $0.51 beats by $0.06, revenue of $104.13M misses by $0.79M
data.description:
• StellarOne press release (NYSE:STEL): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.51 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $104.13M (-2.5% Y/Y) misses by $0.79M.
  • The tax equivalent net interest margin, excluding purchase accounting accretion (“PAA”), was 3.95%(1) for the second quarter of 2025 compared to 3.97%(1) for the first quarter of 2025.
  • Strong Capital Position and Book Value Build: Total risk-based capital ratio increased to 15.98% at June 30, 2025, while book value per share increased to $31.20 at June 30, 2025 from $30.89 at March 31, 2025 and tangible book value per share increased to $19.94(1) at June 30, 2025 from $19.69(1) at March 31, 2025.
  • Low Net Charge-offs: Net charge-offs of $370 thousand, or 0.01% of average loans, for the six months ended June 30, 2025 along with manageable asset quality, compared to $713 thousand, or 0.02% of average loans, for the six months ended June 30, 2024.
  • Repurchase of Shares: Repurchased 791 thousand shares at a weighted average price per share of $26.08 during the second quarter of 2025.

data.title: XPeng delivers its first EV made in Indonesia as global localization strategy launches
data.description:
XPeng (NYSE:XPEV) announced on Friday that it reached a major milestone by delivering its first locally produced X9 model at the 2025 Gaikindo Indonesia International Auto Show. The delivery followed the opening of the Chinese electric vehicle maker's inaugural overseas production base in Indonesia. The event marks Indonesia as a pivotal location in XPeng's (NYSE:XPEV) strategy for global localization, as the company looks to produce intelligent electric vehicles to fit individual Southeast Asian markets. XPeng's (XPEV) investments in Indonesian manufacturing and talent reflect its long-term pledge to expand globally through innovation and tailored local solutions.

"Indonesia embraces some of the world's most respected manufacturing ambitions. Our investment here building facilities, nurturing talent, reflects our commitment to bringing advanced technologies to Indonesia," stated XPeng (XPEV) CEO Xiaopeng He.

XPeng (XPEV) also introduced the New G6 at the 2025 Gaikindo Indonesia International Auto Show, featuring cutting-edge processor technology that was said to enhance AI performance and graphics. The G6 features 800V architecture and 5C ultra-fast charging capabilities that allow charging from 10% to 80% in 12 minutes.

Shares of XPeng (XPEV) slipped 0.9% in premarket trading. The EV stock is up more than 60% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Charter Communications GAAP EPS of $9.18 misses by $0.48, revenue of $13.77B in-line
data.description:
• Charter Communications press release (NASDAQ:CHTR): Q2 GAAP EPS of $9.18 misses by $0.48.
  • Revenue of $13.77B (+0.6% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Second quarter revenue of $13.8 billion grew by 0.6% year-over-year, driven by residential mobile service revenue growth of 24.9%, residential Internet revenue growth of 2.8% and other revenue growth of 18.9%.
  • Second quarter total Internet customers decreased by 117,000. As of June 30, 2025, Charter served 29.9 million Internet customers.
  • Second quarter total mobile lines increased by 500,000. As of June 30, 2025, Charter served 10.9 million mobile lines.
  • As of June 30, 2025, customer relationships totaled 31.2 million, excluding mobile-only relationships.

data.title: Gorman-Rupp Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 misses by $0.02, revenue of $179M beats by $4.4M
data.description:
• Gorman-Rupp press release (NYSE:GRC): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $179M (+5.6% Y/Y) beats by $4.4M.
  • The Company’s backlog of orders was $224.4 million at June 30, 2025 compared to $206.0 million at December 31, 2024, and $224.4 million at June 30, 2024.
